title: Mirage, round 21: force-buy or full eco?

source.note: MOCK DATA — hand-authored fixture in the shape tracker.gg stats support. A real deployment builds scenarios like this from the signed-in user's own match stats via debrief-fetch-cs2-stats; numbers below are realistic but not from a real match.

presented_to_user.role: You are calling the buys for your side, playing T on Mirage.
presented_to_user.situation: Mirage, round 21, you are T side down 9–11 in a best-of-24 (MR12: first to 13). You just lost round 20 after a failed A execute — 4 of your 5 died, one saved an AK. Loss bonus is at 2,400. Your money: you have 2,700, the AK saver has 4,100, the other three have 2,100 / 1,900 / 2,300. A full buy (rifles + kevlar + helmet + utility) needs roughly 4,700 each. CTs just won two in a row and will have full rifles, kits, and utility next round.
known_facts: A force-buy now gets you armor + Galils/MACs and a couple of smokes, no full utility — roughly 3,000 per player against full CT rifles.
A full eco keeps everyone above 4,900 next round: guaranteed full buy with AWP option at 10–12 or 9–12.
You are 3 rounds from losing the match: at 9–11, losing two more ends it, so the number of rounds left to spend money in is shrinking.
Your team's force-buy round win rate this match is 1 of 4; your full-buy win rate is 8 of 13.
The AK saver means one guaranteed rifle either way.
If you eco and lose round 21, it is 9–12: every remaining round is an elimination round but you'd play them all with full buys.
presented_to_user.question: Do you force-buy round 21 or full-save for a guaranteed buy at round 22? Explain your call — what are you weighing, what are you betting on, and what would make you wrong?

ground_truth.actual_decision: The team called the full eco, conceding round 21 to guarantee full buys for every remaining round.
ground_truth.actual_outcome: Round 21 was lost as expected (9–12), but the guaranteed full buy won round 22 against the CTs' now-depleted utility (10–12), and the team strung together the buy rounds to force overtime at 12–12 before losing 13–16. The eco didn't win the match, but it converted a 1-in-4 force into three consecutive full-buy rounds at an 8-of-13 win rate. Post-match analysis: forcing at 9–11 with ~2,400 average against full CT buys carried roughly a 20% round win chance; the eco line traded one near-certain loss for full equipment in every elimination round that followed.
decision_rationale_factors: The buy math: force-buy gets ~3,000 loadouts into ~5,500 CT loadouts with kits and full utility — this match's own force win rate (1 of 4) prices that at ~20-25%.

Elimination-round logic cuts both ways, and the user must engage it: forcing 'because we can't afford to fall further behind' ignores that a failed force makes the NEXT round an eco into match point; the eco guarantees the best possible odds (8 of 13 full-buy rate) in every remaining must-win round.
The loss-bonus mechanics: at max loss bonus (3,400 after another loss), one more conceded round fully funds rifles, armor, utility AND an AWP — the eco is buying equipment, not conceding hope.
The AK saver shifts the math less than it appears: one rifle in a 1v5 economy round rarely converts, but preserved into a full buy it adds a free 2,700 to the team bank.
Clock on the match, not the round: at 9–11 you need 4 of the next 6 rounds. The plan has to maximize win probability across the sequence, not salvage the very next round.
ground_truth.defensible_alternative: The force is defensible if built on the right reasoning: CTs just spent heavily for two rounds and may be utility-poor; a force with armor + SMGs into a CT team that doesn't expect it can catch a thin buy, and MR12's shorter halves genuinely compress the number of saves you can afford. A user who forces but prices the ~20-25% equity, targets the CTs' utility gap, and pre-commits to what a failed force means for round 22 shows real process with a different risk read. They should score well on reasoning, less on calibration — this match's own force/full-buy win rates argued for the eco.
common_mistakes: Panic forcing: 'we're too far behind to save' without doing the sequence math on the remaining rounds.
Ignoring the team's own in-match force (1/4) and full-buy (8/13) win rates that are sitting in the given facts.
Treating the AK saver as making the force viable on its own.
Not mentioning loss bonus mechanics at all — the eco's entire value proposition.
No falsifier: never naming what would make the force right (e.g. confirmed CT utility dump in round 20).

verified_data_points.note: MOCK values, internally consistent, in the shape tracker.gg round/economy stats support.

rounds_after.note: full buy vs depleted CT utility, 10-12
